red_baseline.reason: T025 isolates canonical Skill trace publication and unsafe public route removal to T026.
red_baseline.summary: 6/8 pass; canonical trace markers are absent and five force/risky-clean executable command spans remain.

validation.summary: 8/8 ordered routing, fallback, refusal, recovery, and forbidden-call assertions pass.
Canonical Skill package is valid; the initial Windows default-GBK invocation was rerun in explicit UTF-8 mode because the supported frontmatter includes Chinese triggers.
15/15 installer and Skill routing tests pass.

validation.command: npm run check; git diff --check; npm test
validation.summary: Syntax passes for 100 files; whitespace validation is clean; full suite passes 272 total, 271 passed, 1 existing POSIX skip, 0 failures.

review.evidence: One canonical reference-driven Skill publishes the deterministic trace contract, selects MCP first, permits CLI only before dispatch, treats refusal/uncertainty as terminal for fallback, and exposes no public force/risky/follow/adoption-apply execution route.

obligation_evidence.evidence: The single canonical launchdeck-agent Skill is intent-driven, MCP-first, and reference-based.
Post-dispatch loss uses only known-ID or one bounded journal correlation; no CLI replay is permitted.
Fallback is explicitly limited to pre-dispatch transport/capability absence.
Raw command/env/cwd/force and stronger process-control routes are absent from public Skill execution.
Host hints and model/user approval never elevate Kernel authority.
Uncertain work is never reissued; operation get/list/reconcile are the only recovery calls.
Zero/ambiguous correlation terminates unresolved and cannot trigger fallback.
Machine-readable traces record actual call order, surface, operation, and outcome.

recovery: The Skill validator inherited the Windows GBK locale and could not decode valid UTF-8 Chinese triggers. Explicit Python UTF-8 mode passed, followed by focused and full unchanged validation.
